# Copilot Instructions

- **Repo layout**: Express + ws backend in digital-noticeboard-backend, CRA frontend in digital-noticeboard-frontend. Root package.json only adds shared React deps.
- **Backend server**: [digital-noticeboard-backend/server.js](digital-noticeboard-backend/server.js#L1-L63) builds an HTTP server with WebSocket support; mounts `/api/notices` twice (one static path for local uploads, one absolute) and serves `/uploads` from both `./uploads` and `/home/rohithvarsan/uploads`.
- **Data store**: Notices persist as JSON in [digital-noticeboard-backend/data/notices.json](digital-noticeboard-backend/data/notices.json). Updates write the whole file; no database layer.
- **Notice API**: [digital-noticeboard-backend/routes/notices.js](digital-noticeboard-backend/routes/notices.js#L1-L98) exposes `GET /api/notices`, `POST /api/notices` (multipart field `image`, required `title|content|type`, optional `link`), and `DELETE /api/notices/:id`. IDs are sequential `NGPDNB#####`; images are stored under `/home/rohithvarsan/uploads` and returned as `https://ngpdnb.infaverse.com//uploads/<file>`. No auth/validation beyond required fields.
- **WebSocket fan-out**: The router broadcasts the full notices list on create/delete via the `wss` stored on the Express app; new clients receive the full list on connect from [digital-noticeboard-backend/server.js](digital-noticeboard-backend/server.js#L27-L59). Keep this in sync if adding routes.
- **Frontend routing**: [digital-noticeboard-frontend/src/App.jsx](digital-noticeboard-frontend/src/App.jsx#L1-L46) defines `/` (display board), `/login`, and `/admin` (guarded by `PrivateRoute`). Unknown paths redirect to `/`.
- **Display board**: [digital-noticeboard-frontend/src/components/DisplayBoard.jsx](digital-noticeboard-frontend/src/components/DisplayBoard.jsx#L1-L125) connects to `wss://ngpdnb.infaverse.com/ws/` for real-time notices, cycles through items every 10s with a progress bar, and renders Markdown-style `**bold**` via `dangerouslySetInnerHTML`. A fallback notice uses `uploads/no_notice.jpg` if no data.
- **Admin dashboard**: [digital-noticeboard-frontend/src/components/Dashboard.jsx](digital-noticeboard-frontend/src/components/Dashboard.jsx#L1-L240) manages notices with axios against `https://ngpdnb.infaverse.com/api/notices`. Requires title/category/description/image; images are limited client-side to 1MB; uses FormData `image` field. Delete uses the same base URL. A crawler button hits `/api/crawler` (no backend route present). Inline CSS lives inside the component.
- **Auth model**: [digital-noticeboard-frontend/src/components/Login.jsx](digital-noticeboard-frontend/src/components/Login.jsx#L1-L150) authenticates purely on the client using `process.env.REACT_APP_USERS` (JSON stringified array of `{ email, password }`). Success sets `localStorage.isAuthenticated` and `currentUser`; `PrivateRoute` only checks that flag. No tokens are used despite an axios interceptor.
- **Axios wrapper**: [digital-noticeboard-frontend/src/services/api.js](digital-noticeboard-frontend/src/services/api.js#L1-L16) sets `baseURL` to `https://ngpdnb.infaverse.com:5000/api` and adds an Authorization header from `localStorage.token`, but the dashboard currently bypasses it and talks directly to the hard-coded API URL.
- **Build/run**: Backend has no npm scripts; run from digital-noticeboard-backend with `node server.js` (requires `/home/rohithvarsan/uploads` to exist and be writable). Frontend uses CRA scripts (`npm start`, `npm run build`, `npm test`) from digital-noticeboard-frontend; set `REACT_APP_USERS='[{"email":"admin@example.com","password":"secret"}]'` before `npm start`.
- **Styling**: Most component styling is inlined via `<style>` blocks (dashboard/login) or CSS modules under src/components (display board). Tailwind deps are present but unused.
- **Common pitfalls**: API URLs are hard-coded to production domains; adjust if running locally. WebSocket clients expect the server’s `wss` to sit on the same host/port as HTTP and send the full notices array. Uploaded image paths and double slashes in the returned URL are intentional for the deployed host.
- **When extending**: Keep WebSocket broadcasts in sync after any mutation routes; persist changes via `saveNotices` to the JSON file; honor the `image` multipart field name. Mirror client-side auth expectations if adding protected admin UI, or add real backend auth and update axios usage accordingly.
